author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Luke x. 2, ‘The harvest truly is plenteous,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke x. 2, ‘The harvest truly is plenteous,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the lesson of the Gospel which has just been read, we are reminded to search what that harvest is of which the Lord says, “The harvest truly is great, but the labourers are few.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Pray ye the Lord of the harvest, that He would send forth labourers into His harvest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then to His twelve disciples, whom He also named Apostles, He added other seventy-two, and sent them all, as appears from His words, to the harvest then ready.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then was that harvest?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that harvest was not among these Gentiles, among whom there had been nothing sown.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It remains therefore that we understand that this harvest was among the people of the Jews.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was to that harvest that the Lord of the harvest came, to that harvest He sent reapers; but to the Gentiles He sent not reapers, but sowers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Understand we then that it was harvest among the people of the Jews, sowing time among the peoples of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For out of that harvest were the Apostles chosen, where now that the harvest was, the corn was already ripe; for there had the Prophets sown.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Delightful it is to take a view of God’s husbandry, and to feel delight in His gifts, and the labourers in His field.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in this husbandry did he labour, who said, “I laboured more than they all.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the strength to labour was given him by the Lord of the harvest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore he added, “Yet it is not I, but the grace of God which is with me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that he was employed in this husbandry he clearly enough shows, where he says, “I have planted, Apollos watered.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this Apostle, from Saul, becoming Paul, that is, from being proud, the least of all (for the name of Saul is derived from Saul; but Paul is little; whence in a way interpreting his own name, he says, “I am the least of the Apostles”: this Paul I say, the little, and the least, sent unto the Gentiles, says that he was sent particularly to the Gentiles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He himself so writes, we read, believe, preach it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then in his Epistle to the Galatians says, that having been now called by the Lord Jesus, he came to Jerusalem, and “communicated the Gospel” unto the Apostles, that their right hands were given to him, the sign of harmony, the sign of agreement, that what they had learnt from him differed in no respect from them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Afterwards he says that it was agreed between him and them, that he should go to the Gentiles, and they unto the circumcision, he as a sower, they as reapers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So also with good reason, though they knew it not, did the Athenians give him his name.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as they heard the word from him, they said, “Who is this sower of words?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend then and be it your delight with me to take a view of the husbandry of God and the two harvests in it, the one already past, the other yet to come; the one already past among the people of the Jews, the one yet to come among the peoples of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us prove this; and whereby, but by the Scripture of God, the Lord of the harvest?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See we have it said there in this present lesson, “The harvest is great, but the labourers are few.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Pray ye the Lord of the harvest, that He would send forth labourers into His harvest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because in that harvest there were to be gainsaying and persecuting Jews, He says, “Behold, I send you forth as lambs among wolves.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us show something clearer still touching this harvest in the Gospel according to John, where the Lord sat as He was wearied at the well, great mysteries indeed were transacted, but the time is too short to treat of them all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But give ye ear to that which relates to the present subject.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we have undertaken to show a harvest among the people, among whom the Prophets preached; for therefore were they sowers, that the Apostles might be reapers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A woman of Samaria talks with the Lord Jesus, and when the Lord among other things had told her how God ought to be worshipped, she says, “We know that Messias cometh who is called Christ, and He will teach us all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord saith to her, I that speak with thee am He.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Believe what thou hearest; why dost thou make search for what thou seest?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I that speak with thee am He.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But as to what she had said, “We know that the Messias will come,” whom Moses and the Prophets have announced, “who is called Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The harvest was already in the ear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When it had yet to grow it had received the Prophets as sowers, now that it was come to ripeness it waited for the Apostles as reapers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Presently as she heard this she believed and left her water-pot, and ran in haste, and began to announce the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The disciples at that time had gone to buy bread; who on their return found the Lord talking with the woman, and they marvelled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet did they not dare to say to Him, “What or why talkest Thou with her?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They had astonishment in themselves, they repressed their boldness in their heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To this Samaritan woman then the Name of Christ was nothing new, she was already waiting for His coming, already did she believe that He would come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence had she believed it, if Moses had not sown?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But hear this more expressly noted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord then said to His disciples, “Ye say that the summer is yet far distant, lift up your eyes, and see the fields white already to harvest” And then He adds, “Others have laboured, and ye are entered into their labours.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Abraham laboured, Isaac, Jacob, Moses, the Prophets laboured in sowing; at the Lord’s coming the harvest was found ripe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The reapers sent with the scythe of the Gospel, carried the sheaves into the Lord’s floor, where Stephen was to be threshed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But here comes in that Paul, and he is sent to the Gentiles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this he does not conceal in setting forth the grace, which he had specially and peculiarly received.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he says in his Scriptures, that he was sent to preach the Gospel where Christ had not been named.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because that first harvest was past already, and all the Jews who remained are no harvest, let us consider that harvest which we ourselves are.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it has been sown by Apostles and Prophets.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord Himself sowed it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He was in the Apostles, seeing that Christ also Himself reaped it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they are nothing without Him; He is perfect without them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He saith Himself to them, “For without Me, ye can do nothing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then doth Christ from henceforth sowing among the Gentiles say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “A sower went out to sow.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “There” are reapers “sent out” to reap, “here” an unwearied sower “went out” to sow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what fear did it cause him, that “some seed fell on the way side, and some on rocky places, and some among thorns”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he had been afraid of these unmanageable grounds, he would never have got to the good ground.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is it to us, what affair of ours is it to be disputing now of the Jews, and talking of the chaff? this only concerns us, that we be not “the way side,” nor “the rock,” nor “the thorns,” but “the good ground.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be our heart well-prepared, that from it may come the “thirty,” or the “sixty fold,” or the thousand, and the “hundred fold;” some more, some less; but all is wheat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let it not be “the way side,” where the enemy as a bird may take away the seed trodden down by the passers by.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let it not be “the rock,” where the shallow soil makes it spring up immediately, so that it cannot bear the sun.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let it not be the “thorns,” the lusts of this world, the anxieties of an ill-ordered life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is worse than that anxiety of life, which doth not suffer one to attain unto Life?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more miserable, than by caring for life, to lose Life?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more unhappy, than by fearing death, to fall into death?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the thorns be rooted up, the field prepared, the seeds put in, let them grow unto the harvest, let the barn be longed for, not the fire feared.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: My place accordingly it is, whom with all my unworthiness the Lord hath appointed to be a labourer in His field, to say these things to you, to sow, to plant, to water, yea to dig round about some trees, and to apply the basket of dung; belongeth it to me to do these things faithfully; to you to receive them faithfully; to the Lord to aid me in my labour, and you in your belief, all of us labouring, but in Him overcoming the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then belongs to your place I have already said; now I wish to say what belongs to ours.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But peradventure it seems to some of you, that it is something superfluous which I have declared that I wish to say, and speaking within themselves they are saying in thought, “O that he would now let us go!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He has said already what belongs to our place, as to that which belongs to his, what is that to us?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I think it is better that in a reciprocal and mutual love, we should belong to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are now indeed of one family, we of the same family are dispensers, it is true, but we all belong to one Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nor what I give, do I give of mine own; but of His from whom I also receive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if I should give of mine own, I shall give a lie.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For he that speaketh a lie, speaketh of his own.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then ye ought to give ear to that which belongs to the duty of the dispenser, whether it be that ye may have joy in yourselves, if ye find yourselves to be such, or whether it be that ye may be even in this very thing instructed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how many are there among this people who shall some day be dispensers!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I too was once where ye now are; and I who am seen now to be measuring out to my fellowservants their food from this higher place, a few years since in a lower place was receiving food with my fellow-servants.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am speaking now a Bishop to lay-men; but I know that in speaking to them I am speaking to many who will some day be bishops also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us see then how we must understand what the Lord enjoined on them whom He sent to preach the Gospel, and let us consider in our mind this prepared harvest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Carry,” He saith, “neither purse, nor scrip, nor shoes; and salute no man by the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And into whatsoever house ye enter, say, Peace be to this house.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the Son of peace be there, your peace shall rest upon it; if not, it shall return to you again.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If it hath “rested,” hath the other lost it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This be far from the mind of Saints!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then this is not to be taken in a carnal sense; and hence it may be neither are the “purse,” nor “shoes,” nor “scrip;” nor above all that, where if we take it simply without examination, pride seems to be enjoined us, that we “salute no man by the way.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us give heed to our Lord, our True Example and Succour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us prove that He is our Succour; “Without Me ye can do nothing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us prove that He is our Example; “Christ,” says Peter, “suffered for us, leaving us an example that we should follow His steps.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our Lord Himself had bags in the way, and these bags He entrusted to Judas.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is true He suffered from the thief; but I as desiring to learn of my Lord say, “O Lord, Thou didst suffer from the thief, whence hadst Thou that of which he could take away?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Me, a wretched and infirm man Thou hast admonished not even to carry a purse; Thou didst carry bags, and hadst that in which Thou couldest suffer from the thief.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If Thou hadst not carried them, neither could he have found anything to take away.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What remains, but that he here saith to me, “Understand what that thou hearest, ‘Carry no purse,’ means?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is a purse?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Money shut up, that is, concealed wisdom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, ‘Carry no purse?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be not wise within your own selves only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Receive ye the Holy Ghost.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It should be a fountain in thee, not a purse; from whence distribution is made to others, not where it is itself shut in.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the scrip is the same as the purse.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What are “the shoes”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The shoes which we use, are the skins of dead beasts, the coverings of our feet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By this then are we bidden to renounce dead works.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This Moses was admonished of in a figure, when the Lord speaking to him said, “Loose thy shoes from off thy feet; for the place wherein thou standest is holy ground.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What ground is so holy as the Church of God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In it therefore let us stand, let us loose our shoes, let us, that is, renounce dead works.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as touching these shoes, wherewith we walk, the same my Lord again assures me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if He had not been shod Himself, John would not have said of Him, “I am not worthy to unloose the latchet of His shoes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be there obedience then, let not a haughty severity steal over us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I,” says one, “fulfil the Gospel, because I walk with naked feet.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well, thou canst do it, I cannot.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let us both keep that which we both receive together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us glow with charity, let us love one another; and so it shall be, that I will love your strength, and thou shall bear my weakness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what thinkest thou, who dost not choose to understand in what sense these words are used, and who art forced by thy perverse interpretation to slander even the Lord Himself as to the “bags” and “shoes;” what thinkest thou?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Does it please thee then, that as we meet our friends in the way, we should neither pay them our salutations if they are our betters, nor return the salutations of our inferiors?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What, dost thou fulfil the Gospel, because thou art saluted, and art silent?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thus thou wilt not be like to the traveller going on the way, but to the milestone pointing out the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then lay aside this coarse interpretation, and understand aright the words of the Lord, “and salute no man by the way.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not without a cause that we are enjoined this, nor would He mislike us to do what He enjoined.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is, “Salute no man by the way”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It might indeed be even simply taken thus, that He has commanded us to do what He enjoins with all speed; and that His words “Salute no man by the way,” are as though He had said, “Put all other things by, till ye accomplish what has been enjoined you;” according to that style of speaking by which expressions are wont to be exaggerated in the custom of conversation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nor need we go far; in the same discourse a little while afterwards He says, “And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted to heaven, shall be thrust down to hell.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “exalted to heaven”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did the walls of that city touch the clouds, or reach to the stars?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what is “exalted to heaven”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou seemest thyself to be surpassing happy, surpassing powerful, thou art exceeding proud.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As then for the sake of exaggeration this was said, “Thou art exalted unto heaven” to that city, which was not exalted, nor rose up unto heaven; so to express haste hyperbolically was it said, “So run, so do what I have enjoined you, that travellers by the way may not in the least retard you; but disregarding all things else, hasten to the end set before you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But there is another more recondite meaning in these words which it is not difficult to understand, which respects more particularly myself and all dispensers, and you too who are hearers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that salutes, wishes salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so the ancients in their letters wrote thus, “Such a one sends salvation to another.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Salutation derives its name from this salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is, “Salute no man by the way”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who “salute by the way,” do so “by occasion.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I see that ye have quickly understood me, yet for all that I must not finish yet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye have not all understood so quickly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have seen that some understand by their voice, I see more asking for something further by their silence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But seeing that we are talking of the way, let us walk as it were in the way: ye quick ones, wait for the slow, and walk evenly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then did I say, He “who salutes by the way,” salutes only by occasion?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was not going to him whom he salutes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was about one thing, another came in his way; he was seeking one thing, he found across his path some other thing to do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is it to “salute by occasion”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “By occasion” to announce salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what else is it to announce salvation, but to preach the Gospel?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then thou dost preach, do it by love, and not “by occasion.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are men then, who though “they seek their own things,” yet preach no other Gospel; of whom the Apostle says with sighing, “For all seek their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And these “saluted,” that is announced salvation, they preached the Gospel; but they sought some other thing, and therefore they saluted only “by occasion.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what is this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou art such an one, whosoever thou art, thou doest it; nay not all of you who do it are such, but it may be that some of you who do it are.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou art such, it is not that thou doest it, but it is done by thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For such as these did the Apostle suffer; yet did he not enjoin them so to be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And these do something, or something is done by them; they seek something else, yet they preach the word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Care not what the preacher seeks after; be it thy will to hold fast what he preaches; but let his intention be no concern of thine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the word of salvation from his mouth, from his mouth hold fast this salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be not thou the judge of his heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou seest that he is seeking after other things, what is that to thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear Him who is Salvation; “What they say, do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He has given thee assurance who hath said, “What they say, do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do they evil?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Do not what they do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do they good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They do not “salute by the way,” they do not preach the Gospel by occasion; “be ye followers of them, even as they also are of Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A good man preaches to thee; pluck the grape from the vine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A bad man preaches to thee, pluck the grape as it hangs in the hedge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The cluster has grown on the vine-branch entangled among the thorns, but it has not grown from the thorns.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By all means when thou seest any such thing as this and art hungry, be careful as thou pluckest it, lest when thou puttest forth thy hand to the grape, thou be torn by the thorns.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is what I say; in such wise hear what is good, as that thou imitate not the evil of the character.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him preach “by occasion,” salute by the way; it will injure him because he has not given ear to the precept of Christ, “Salute no man by the way;” it will not injure thee, who, whether thou dost hear of salvation from a passer by, or from one who comes direct to thee, dost hold fast that salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle, who as I have said already gives us to understand this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “What then?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “So that in every way, whether by occasion or in truth, Christ is preached; and herein I do rejoice, yea, and will rejoice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I know that this shall turn to my salvation through your prayer.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let then such as these, the Apostles of Christ, the preachers of the Gospel, who “salute not by the way,” that is, who do not seek or do any other thing, but who in genuine charity preach the Gospel, let them come into the house, and say, “Peace to this house.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They speak not with the mouth only; they pour out that of which they are full; they preach peace, and they have peace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are not as those of whom it was said, “Peace, Peace, and there is no peace.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “Peace, Peace, and there is no peace”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They preach it, but they have it not; they praise it and they love it not; they say, and do not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But yet do thou receive the peace, “whether by occasion or in truth Christ be preached.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso then is full of peace, and salutes, saying, “Peace to this house, if the son of peace be there, his peace shall rest upon him; if not,” for peradventure there is no one of peace there, yet he who saluted has lost nothing, “it shall return,” says he, “to you again.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It shall return to thee, though it never departed from thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this He would mean to say, It profiteth thee that thou hast declared it, it hath not profited him at all who hath not received it; thou hast not lost thy reward, because he hath remained empty; it is rendered thee for thy good will, it is rendered thee for the charity which thou hast bestowed, He will render it to thee who hath given thee assurance of it by that Angelic voice, “Peace on earth to men of good will.”
